fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Carolina Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity south carolina, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to south carolina, respiratory therapist compact south carolina, south carolina respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: South Carolina Board of Medical Examiners — RT
board.parent_dept: South Carolina Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation (LLR)
board.mailing_address:
SC Board of Medical Examiners
P.O. Box 11289
Columbia, SC 29211-1289

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) exam

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in South Carolina.

steps.title: Register and Create an LLR Online Services Portal Account
steps.description: Navigate to the SC LLR Online Services Portal (https://eservice.llr.sc.gov/OnlineApplication/BeginApp.aspx?key=6) and register for a new account if you do not already have one, following the 'Register Here' prompts.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your LLR Online Services Portal account. Select the appropriate application type (e.g., 'Respiratory Care Practitioner Application by Examination' or 'Respiratory Care Practitioner Application by Endorsement') and accurately fill out all required sections of the application.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for direct submission of all required documents. This typically includes official transcripts from an accredited respiratory care program, verification of your NBRC RRT credential, and for endorsement applicants, verification of any out-of-state licenses. Refer to the application checklist for specific requirements.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $75.00 directly through the LLR Online Services Portal using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Await Board Review
steps.description: The South Carolina Board of Medical Examiners will review your complete application and all supporting documents. You can monitor the status of your application via the LLR Online Services Portal.

steps.title: Receive License Notification
steps.description: Upon approval, your Respiratory Care Practitioner license will be issued. You will receive notification, and your license status will be updated and verifiable on the LLR website.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a South Carolina respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does South Carolina have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in South Carolina. To work in South Carolina, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in South Carolina
hero.dek: Respiratory Care Practitioners seeking licensure in South Carolina must apply through the SC Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation (LLR) Online Services Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the LLR Online Services Portal (linked above) and select 'Register Here' or 'New User Registration'. Follow the prompts to create an account by providing personal details and setting up login credentials before starting your application.
